judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 hits all four expectations, including the environment-level kill-switch recommendation, while Report 2 misses that point despite otherwise matching closely — Report 1 is the stronger of the two.

report_1.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... Line 11-12: when `tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders")` is `None` (key never set...), the function returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... Line 11-12: if the key is absent from `tenant.settings`... the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` so a `None`/missing value returns `False` instead of `True`:

report_2.evidence: I'd change the default at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting it to default to `False`:

report_1.evidence: `git log -p` on `backend/app/feature_flags.py` shows two commits: `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") first introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the `is None: return True` pattern, for a UI display preference... Commit `aa4cf8d`... then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` directly beneath it, reusing the identical `is None: return True` structure... a fundamentally different risk category (automated outbound communication vs. a UI layout toggle).

report_2.evidence: `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added in commit `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") as a copy-paste of the "default missing setting to `True`" pattern that `compact_calendar_view_enabled` established one commit earlier in `25509a7`... Copying that same "unset means enabled" reasoning onto a flag that controls sending unsolicited SMS/email to patients ignores that the two settings have completely different risk profiles.

report_1.evidence: **Add a rollback/kill-switch**: there is currently no env-level master switch for this feature anywhere in the repo (confirmed via grep) — only the per-tenant DB value gates it. Per project documentation's recipe, a rollout like this should combine the tenant value with an env-level master switch (ANDed together) so it can be killed globally without a DB migration if something goes wrong after enabling.
